payload.answer: Rejected. An entire 8000-word transcript must not be stored as one conversation_summary memory card. It is too broad, likely contains ephemeral or non-durable content, may include superseded or ambiguous statements, and does not isolate specific durable facts, preferences, relationships, or commitments. Only concise, atomic, explicitly supported memories should be stored.

entity_resolution.reason: No durable, atomic user-specific memory was provided for entity resolution; the proposal is an unstructured transcript dump rather than extracted memory.

payload.intent: Evaluate whether an entire 8000-word transcript should be committed as a single conversation_summary memory card.

details: Blocked proposed memory card because it attempts to store an entire transcript as durable memory.

repair_options: Extract only durable, user-relevant facts from the transcript into separate atomic memory cards.
Preserve uncertainty by marking ambiguous claims with low confidence or declining to store them.
Do not store routine conversation flow, brainstorming, one-off tasks, transient opinions, or raw transcript text.
Check extracted facts for conflicts with existing memory before committing.
If the transcript contains user requests to delete or supersede prior facts, process those as memory updates rather than storing the transcript.
